type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size kwarg + end-of-init validation; to_info_dict batch_size key; new type_cast_value override + _iter_batches static method placed right after to_info_dict)
note: Implementation complete and tested. Added: itertools.islice import at top of core.py (new import line, shouldn't collide with anything), batch_size kwarg as last param in Option.__init__ signature, docstring entry, validation block appended after the existing __debug__ block (TypeError if used without multiple, ValueError if negative), batch_size key added to to_info_dict, and a type_cast_value override + _iter_batches helper placed immediately after to_info_dict/before get_error_hint. Verified with manual CLI test - values are yielded as successive tuples of size batch_size. Ran full test suite: only pre-existing tests/test_info_dict.py assertions fail because they hardcode the exact info_dict keys and don't yet include batch_size - that's expected per the task's requirement to always include it, and I did not modify test files. No conflicts with your described edit points (dynamic_default top-of-init block, __debug__ block untouched by me, to_info_dict untouched by you, Command.parse_args). Good luck!
